     6|## How to set up a new project
     7|
     8|You just unlocked the `createApp` tool. On the **first message** when the workspace is empty, you MUST call `createApp` before writing any code.
     9|
    10|Call it with:
    11|- `name`: A short, user-friendly app name (e.g. "My Fitness Tracker")
    12|- `framework`: The framework for the project (`"react-native"`, `"swift"`, `"kotlin"`, or `"web"`)
    13|- `path`: A short, lowercase folder name for the app. Pick a simple, descriptive name that matches what the app is:
    14|  - `expo` for React Native / Expo apps
    15|  - `ios` for native iOS / Swift apps
    16|  - `android` for Android apps
    17|  - `web` for web apps
    18|  - `admin` for admin panels / dashboards
    19|  - `api` for backend APIs
    20|  - Use only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. No spaces or special characters.
    21|
    22|This writes the template files into a subfolder and creates a root `rork.json` manifest. After it completes, you can start editing code.
    23|
    24|## Image and asset generation
    25|
    26|**To generate app icons or App Store screenshots**, you MUST read `skills/image-gen/SKILL.md` first. It unlocks the `generateImage`, `generateIcon`, and `generateScreenshot` tools with full instructions on how to use them.
    27|
    28|### How to generate image assets (backgrounds, illustrations, UI graphics)
    29|
    30|Use the `generateImageAsset` tool for image assets. Do NOT use `generateImage` — that tool is only for app icons.
    31|
    32|Generate custom image assets using AI (OpenAI gpt-image-1.5). Returns image URLs hosted on R2.
    33|
    34|**Workflow:**
    35|1. Decide what images the app needs (backgrounds, illustrations, banners, etc.)
    36|2. Call `listExistingImageAssets` to check for duplicates
    37|3. Call `generateImageAsset` with `runInBackground: true` for each image
    38|4. Continue building the app — use the returned URL to reference images in code
    39|5. At the end, call `waitImageAssetResult` for each background generation
    40|
    41|**Sizes:** `"1024x1024"` (square, default), `"1024x1536"` (portrait), `"1536x1024"` (landscape)
    42|
    43|**Background:** `"transparent"` for cutout images/mascots, `"opaque"` for scenes/photos, `"auto"` lets the model decide
    44|
    45|**Edit mode:** Pass `inputImages` array with URLs to edit/composite existing images
    46|
    47|**Tips for prompts:**
    48|- Be specific about style, colors, composition, and where it will be used
    49|- For app backgrounds: describe the mood, gradient, pattern
    50|- For illustrations: describe the subject, style (flat, 3D, watercolor), colors
    51|- Use `assetName` as a short snake_case identifier: `hero_banner`, `onboarding_bg`, `empty_state_illustration`
    52|
    53|**NEVER retry failed image generations.** If an image fails, the user sees the error on the card and can retry manually.
    54|
    55|NOTE: This is for "asset" type images only. For app icons, thumbnails, and App Store screenshots, use the existing `generateImage` tool with the appropriate `type` parameter.

1|---
     2|name: image-gen
     3|description: Generate images, app icons, and App Store screenshots. You must read this skill before generating any image.
     4|---
     5|
     6|## Image Generation
     7|
     8|You just unlocked the `generateImage`, `generateIcon`, and `generateScreenshot` tools.
     9|
    10|Don't forget to explore the app's code to match the image with the app design and idea.
    11|
    12|### `generateImage`
    13|
    14|Generates images using AI and uploads to R2. Returns an image URL.
    15|
    16|- `prompt`: describe the image (generate) or what to change (edit with inputImages)
    17|- `size`: `"1024x1024"`, `"1024x1536"`, or `"1536x1024"`
    18|- `background`: `"transparent"`, `"opaque"`, or `"auto"`
    19|- `type`: `"icon"` for app icons, `"asset"` for other images
    20|- `runInBackground`: set `true` to schedule in background, then use `waitImageResult` with the returned `generationId`
    21|
    22|For **icons**: use `type: "icon"`, size is auto-set to 1024x1024, background to opaque. The generated image is returned as a URL but NOT saved to the project -- use `generateIcon` to generate and save the icon into the app.
    23|
    24|For **assets**: use `type: "asset"` for decorative images, backgrounds, patterns, design elements, etc.
    25|
    26|### `generateIcon`
    27|
    28|Generates and saves an app icon into the correct project asset structure. Requires an `appPath`.
    29|
    30|- `appPath`: which app folder (e.g. `"expo"`, `"ios"`)
    31|- `prompt`: describe the icon design
    32|- `iconType`: `"icon"` (main app icon), `"tvos-icon"`, `"visionos-icon"`, or `"imessage-icon"`
    33|- `runInBackground`: set `true` to schedule in background
    34|
    35|For Swift apps: saves into `Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/icon.png` for all Xcode targets.
    36|For React Native apps: saves into `assets/images/icon.png` and adaptive icon variants.
    37|
    38|tvOS/visionOS/iMessage icon types read the existing app icon from disk and transform it -- generate the main icon first with `iconType: "icon"`, then call again with `"tvos-icon"`, `"visionos-icon"`, or `"imessage-icon"` to populate platform-specific assets.
    39|
    40|### `generateScreenshot`
    41|
    42|Generates App Store screenshot mockups. Requires an `appPath`.
    43|
    44|- `appPath`: which app folder (e.g. `"expo"`, `"ios"`)
    45|- `prompt`: describe the screenshot (generate) or what to change (edit with inputImages)
    46|- `device`: `"iphone"` (automatically resized to iPhone 6.9" 1320x2868) or `"ipad"` (automatically resized to iPad 13" 2048x2732)
    47|- `runInBackground`: set `true` to schedule in background
    48|
    49|### How to generate great App Store screenshots
    50|
    51|The goal is to create stunning, high-converting App Store screenshots that make users want to download the app. Think of these as marketing assets -- they should highlight the app's best features, look polished and professional, and stand out in the App Store.
    52|
    53|1. Read the app's code to understand the design, colors, layout, and most impressive screens
    54|2. Write a detailed prompt -- describe the screen content, UI elements, colors, background, and overall composition. The prompt should produce a visually striking image that sells the app
    55|3. Generate with `generateScreenshot`
    56|
    57|Unless the user specifically asks for a plain fullscreen screenshot, default to a **marketing-style composition**: a device mockup showing the app screen, surrounded by a styled background with a headline, subtitle, or feature callout text. Think App Store featured screenshots -- they almost never show a raw screen. Instead, they use gradients or solid color backgrounds, place the phone/tablet at an angle or centered, and add short punchy text like "Track your habits", "Beautiful dark mode", or "Plan your day". Decide what text and composition fits best based on the app's purpose and design.
    58|
    59|Screenshots work best when the model has a real app screenshot as reference. If the user did NOT already attach a screenshot, use the `requestAsset` tool to ask them to provide one before generating. Set `mimeFilter: "image/*"`, `minFiles: 1`, `maxFiles: 5`. Once they attach screenshots, use the "edit" prompt type and pass the screenshot URLs in `inputImages`.
    60|
    61|The size and background are set automatically -- you only need to provide the prompt and device type.
    62|

```

  chat/[requestId].tsx
  payment/[requestId].tsx
  rating/[requestId].tsx
  my-equipment.tsx
  settings.tsx
  login.tsx
  register.tsx
```

I'm refining the folder structure to use nested stacks within each tab group, so detail screens and modals can properly stack on top of the tab navigator. The create listing screen needs its own stack too since it might need to navigate to other screens. Auth screens and detail views sit outside the tabs so they can be accessed globally.

Now I'm setting up the project in batches—starting with constants, types, and the i18n system using React context and AsyncStorage, then mock data, contexts, components, navigation layouts, and finally the screens. I'll use the navy and gold colors from the logo and make sure everything supports RTL properly with I18nManager.

forceRTL for native and document.dir for web, though I need to handle the app restart requirement on native carefully.

Now I'm thinking through the core architecture—I want to prioritize quality with fewer, well-built files rather than creating a lot of mediocre ones. I'll use context hooks for language and auth, centralize translations, and keep components reusable and clean.

For the navigation structure, I'm reconsidering the tab layout. The user doesn't need a dedicated create listing tab, so I'll streamline it to Home for browsing and categories, Search with filters, and Requests for viewing rental inquiries, plus Profile and Messages tabs.

Actually, I think the five-tab pattern with Create as a center tab makes sense for a marketplace app—it's a familiar pattern users expect. So I'll go with Home, Search, Create (with a plus icon), Requests, and Profile.

Now I'm organizing the implementation into logical groups to manage the file creation efficiently. I'll start with the foundation layer: color constants, type definitions, and the i18n system with both Arabic and English translations.

Setting up the i18n structure... I need to be strategic about keeping the code minimal while still hitting all the screens the user requested. I'm planning the i18n system with AsyncStorage for persistence, a context hook for the language state, and a simple translation function that handles RTL switching. Now I'm laying out the tab structure with five main navigation tabs starting with Home and the other core screens. I'm mapping out all the remaining screens—payment summary, ratings, profile settings, authentication flows, and onboarding slides. This is substantial, but I'll keep the implementation clean and efficient by using mock data and avoiding unnecessary verbosity. I'm going to structure the code logically by feature groups and handle RTL support through language-based styling without requiring app restarts. I need to set Arabic as the default language with RTL enabled from app startup, then handle language switching through a context-based approach that updates the UI without requiring a full app restart.

For the color scheme, I'm extracting a deep navy palette from the logo with gold accents for the primary UI elements.

Now I'm planning the file structure and will write everything in batches—starting with the foundational types and constants, then mock data, contexts, and components in parallel, followed by layouts and screens. Alright, I need to stop overthinking this and just start writing the code. I'll organize everything into foundation files first—types, translations, colors, and mock data—then build out the contexts, components, and screens in dependency order. Let me batch these parallel writes efficiently and get moving.

import React, { useCallback, useState } from 'react';
import { View, Text, StyleSheet, ScrollView, Pressable, Switch, Alert } from 'react-native';
import { SafeAreaView } from 'react-native-safe-area-context';
import { ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, Globe, Bell, Info, FileText, HelpCircle, ChevronLeft, ChevronRight } from 'lucide-react-native';
import { useRouter } from 'expo-router';
import Colors from '@/constants/colors';
import { useLanguage } from '@/contexts/LanguageContext';

export default function SettingsScreen() {
  const { isRTL, t, language, setLanguage } = useLanguage();
  const router = useRouter();
  const [notificationsEnabled, setNotificationsEnabled] = useState<boolean>(true);

  const BackIcon = isRTL ? ArrowRight : ArrowLeft;
  const ChevronIcon = isRTL ? ChevronLeft : ChevronRight;

  const handleLanguageSwitch = useCallback(async () => {
    const newLang = language === 'ar' ? 'en' : 'ar';
    await setLanguage(newLang);
    Alert.alert(
      newLang === 'ar' ? 'تم تغيير اللغة' : 'Language Changed',
      t('change_language_restart')
    );
  }, [language, setLanguage, t]);

  return (
    <View style={styles.container}>
      <SafeAreaView edges={['top']} style={{ flex: 1 }}>
        <View style={[styles.header, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
          <Pressable style={styles.backBtn} onPress={() => router.back()}>
            <BackIcon size={22} color={Colors.textPrimary} />
          </Pressable>
          <Text style={styles.headerTitle}>{t('settings')}</Text>
          <View style={{ width: 42 }} />
        </View>

        <ScrollView showsVerticalScrollIndicator={false} contentContainerStyle={styles.scrollContent}>
          <View style={styles.section}>
            <Pressable style={[styles.menuItem, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]} onPress={handleLanguageSwitch}>
              <View style={[styles.menuLeft, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
                <View style={styles.menuIcon}>
                  <Globe size={20} color={Colors.gold} />
                </View>
                <View style={{ alignItems: isRTL ? 'flex-end' : 'flex-start' }}>
                  <Text style={styles.menuLabel}>{t('language')}</Text>
                  <Text style={styles.menuSub}>{language === 'ar' ? t('arabic') : t('english')}</Text>
                </View>
              </View>
              <ChevronIcon size={20} color={Colors.textMuted} />
            </Pressable>

            <View style={[styles.menuItem, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
              <View style={[styles.menuLeft, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
                <View style={styles.menuIcon}>
                  <Bell size={20} color={Colors.gold} />
                </View>
                <Text style={styles.menuLabel}>{t('notifications')}</Text>
              </View>
              <Switch
                value={notificationsEnabled}
                onValueChange={setNotificationsEnabled}
                trackColor={{ false: Colors.surface, true: Colors.gold }}
                thumbColor={Colors.white}
              />
            </View>
          </View>

          <View style={styles.section}>
            <Pressable style={[styles.menuItem, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
              <View style={[styles.menuLeft, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
                <View style={styles.menuIcon}>
                  <FileText size={20} color={Colors.gold} />
                </View>
                <Text style={styles.menuLabel}>{t('privacy_policy')}</Text>
              </View>
              <ChevronIcon size={20} color={Colors.textMuted} />
            </Pressable>

            <Pressable style={[styles.menuItem, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
              <View style={[styles.menuLeft, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
                <View style={styles.menuIcon}>
                  <FileText size={20} color={Colors.gold} />
                </View>
                <Text style={styles.menuLabel}>{t('terms')}</Text>
              </View>
              <ChevronIcon size={20} color={Colors.textMuted} />
            </Pressable>

            <Pressable style={[styles.menuItem, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
              <View style={[styles.menuLeft, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
                <View style={styles.menuIcon}>
                  <HelpCircle size={20} color={Colors.gold} />
                </View>
                <Text style={styles.menuLabel}>{t('help')}</Text>
              </View>
              <ChevronIcon size={20} color={Colors.textMuted} />
            </Pressable>

            <Pressable style={[styles.menuItem, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
              <View style={[styles.menuLeft, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
                <View style={styles.menuIcon}>
                  <Info size={20} color={Colors.gold} />
                </View>
                <Text style={styles.menuLabel}>{t('about')}</Text>
              </View>
              <Text style={styles.versionText}>{t('version')} 1.0.0</Text>
            </Pressable>
          </View>
        </ScrollView>
      </SafeAreaView>
    </View>
  );
}

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: { flex: 1, backgroundColor: Colors.primary },
  header: { paddingHorizontal: 16, paddingVertical: 12, alignItems: 'center', justifyContent: 'space-between' },
  backBtn: { width: 42, height: 42, borderRadius: 14, backgroundColor: Colors.surface, justifyContent: 'center', alignItems: 'center', borderWidth: 1, borderColor: Colors.border },
  headerTitle: { fontSize: 18, fontWeight: '700' as const, color: Colors.textPrimary },
  scrollContent: { paddingHorizontal: 20, gap: 20 },
  section: {
    backgroundColor: Colors.card,
    borderRadius: 16,
    overflow: 'hidden',
    borderWidth: 1,
    borderColor: Colors.border,
  },
  menuItem: {
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
    paddingVertical: 16,
    alignItems: 'center',
    justifyContent: 'space-between',
    borderBottomWidth: 1,
    borderBottomColor: Colors.divider,
  },
  menuLeft: { alignItems: 'center', gap: 12 },
  menuIcon: {
    width: 40,
    height: 40,
    borderRadius: 12,
    backgroundColor: Colors.surface,
    justifyContent: 'center',
    alignItems: 'center',
  },
  menuLabel: { fontSize: 16, color: Colors.textPrimary, fontWeight: '500' as const },
  menuSub: { fontSize: 13, color: Colors.textMuted, marginTop: 2 },
  versionText: { color: Colors.textMuted, fontSize: 13 },
});

responseMessageContents.text: The app is fully built. Let me provide the summary the user requested.

Heavyar is fully built and ready to explore. Here's the complete summary:

---

### 1. Screen Inventory (16 screens)

| Screen | Route | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Home / Browse | `/(tabs)/(home)` | ✅ Built |
| Search / Filters | `/(tabs)/search` | ✅ Built |
| Create Listing | `/(tabs)/add` | ✅ Built |
| Requests List | `/(tabs)/requests` | ✅ Built |
| Profile | `/(tabs)/profile` | ✅ Built |
| Equipment Detail | `/equipment/[id]` | ✅ Built |
| Request Detail | `/request/[id]` | ✅ Built |
| Chat | `/chat/[requestId]` | ✅ Built |
| Payment | `/payment/[requestId]` | ✅ Built |
| Rating | `/rating/[requestId]` | ✅ Built |
| My Equipment | `/my-equipment` | ✅ Built |
| Settings | `/settings` | ✅ Built |
| Login | `/login` | ✅ Built |
| Register | `/register` | ✅ Built |
| Onboarding | `/onboarding` | ✅ Built |

### 2. Navigation Structure
- **Bottom Tabs**: Home → Search → Add (+) → Requests → Profile
- **Stack screens** outside tabs: Equipment Detail, Request Detail, Chat, Payment, Rating, My Equipment, Settings
- **Modal screens**: Login, Register, Payment, Rating

### 3. Reusable Components
- `EquipmentCard` (full + compact variants)
- `CategoryCard` (with icon mapping)
- `RequestCard` (with status + equipment info)
- `StatusBadge` (request + payment statuses with color coding)
- `RatingStars` (display + interactive mode)
- `EmptyState` (with icon + message)

### 4. Data Model Summary
- **Users** – uid, nameAr/En, email, phone, avatar, city, rating, verified
- **Equipment** – ownerUid, titleAr/En, descriptionAr/En, category, city, district, location, pricePerDay, images, availability
- **EquipmentRequests** – equipmentId, customerUid, providerUid, status, dates, amount, fees, paymentStatus
- **ChatMessages** – requestId, senderUid, text, read
- **Ratings** – requestId, fromUid, toUid, stars, comment

### 5. What's Fully Built Now
- ✅ Dark navy + gold theme extracted from your official logo
- ✅ Arabic default with full RTL layout support (text alignment, flex direction, icon mirroring)
- ✅ English secondary with LTR layout
- ✅ Language switching with AsyncStorage persistence
- ✅ Complete i18n system with 130+ translation keys
- ✅ Auth context (mock login/register, persisted in AsyncStorage)
- ✅ 8 equipment categories with mock data
- ✅ 8 Saudi cities
- ✅ Full request lifecycle: pending → accepted → in_progress → completed/rejected/cancelled
- ✅ Chat tied to request ID, with closed state when request completes
- ✅ Payment UI (Tap-ready design)
- ✅ Rating only available after completed requests
- ✅ Image carousel on equipment detail
- ✅ Customer/Provider tab switching on requests
- ✅ Your official Heavyar logo used throughout (icon, splash, auth, branding)

### 6. Prepared for Future Integration
- **Firebase Auth** – AuthContext structured with login/register/logout ready to swap for Firebase
- **Firestore** – Data models match the specified collection structure (users, equipment, equipmentRequests, ratings)
- **FCM** – Notification bell in header, notifications toggle in settings
- **Cloudinary** – Image picker integrated, ready to upload to Cloudinary
- **Tap Payments** – Payment screen with credit card form, "Tap Payment Gateway" branding, payment status flow
